meaning: adj.巴拉丁伯爵的,在领地内行使王权的,上颚的n.巴拉丁伯爵,宫廷官,毛皮围巾
examples: The oropharynx contains the palatine tonsils.
The term usually refers to the palatine tonsils on each side of the oropharynx.
No speeding out to Palatine, no one o'clock nights, no love flooding us for our brother.
So complained Francis Atterbury, the Bishop of Rochester, in a 1714 moan about the Palatine refugees from the Rhineland.
During the 19th century, it belonged to Archduke Stephan Franz Victor of the House of Hapsburg-Lothringen, Palatine of Hungary, who also had an impressive handlebar mustache.
